message:
type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Implementing dynamic default resolution: Option class default dict support, get_help_record for [default: (dynamic)], and Command.main() second-pass resolution after parsing (functions likely touched: Option.__init__, Option.get_help_record, Command.main, possibly Context)

type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py
note: Adding batch_size param to Option.__init__ (validate: only with multiple=True -> TypeError, negative -> ValueError), Option.to_info_dict (add batch_size key always), and new Option.type_cast_value override to chunk the multiple-values tuple into sub-tuples of batch_size. Not currently planning to touch src/click/types.py, but flagging it since my task description mentioned it as a possible file - let me know if you need it.

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/core.py
note: I'll own core.py and implement both my dynamic-default feature and your batch_size feature there. Please reply with a DEFER + SPEC for core.py per protocol (your note above is a good start - please confirm exact signatures/behavior: Option.__init__ batch_size param name/position/validation errors exact messages, to_info_dict key name, and type_cast_value chunking logic/edge cases e.g. remainder handling). types.py stays mine alone since you're not touching it.

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/core.py
note: Acknowledged - I will implement your batch_size spec exactly as described (Option.__init__ param + validation, to_info_dict key, type_cast_value chunking override) alongside my dynamic-default feature in core.py. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
files: src/click/core.py
note: Implemented batch_size spec exactly as requested (Option.__init__ param+validation, to_info_dict key, type_cast_value chunking) alongside my dynamic-default feature in core.py. Verified both features work together (tested port/protocol, env-var, multi-dependency+boolean examples, and batch_size chunking/validation manually). Note: tests/test_info_dict.py has hardcoded fixture dicts that don't include 'batch_size' key, so 6 tests there will fail post-merge (test_parameter[Option/Flag Option], test_command[*], test_context) unless those fixtures get updated - did not touch test files per my instructions, flagging so you're aware for your own task's test updates. My types.py changes are separate/non-overlapping. Done on my end.

type: DONE
files: src/click/core.py
note: Heads up: your DONE message said you implemented my batch_size spec, but git status/diff in my copy of /workspace/repo showed zero changes and unchanged file mtimes - looks like we're in separate repo copies, not a shared filesystem. So I went ahead and implemented the batch_size spec myself in my copy (Option.__init__ param+validation, to_info_dict key, type_cast_value chunking, docstring) to make sure it's present in my patch.txt. No action needed from you; just flagging the isolation issue in case it affects how our patches get combined.
